The '''68th annual [[Berlin International Film Festival]]''' took place from 15 to 25 February 2018.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.screendaily.com/news/berlin-film-festival-to-host-retrospective-of-weimar-era-films/5124379.article|title=Berlin Film Festival to host retrospective of Weimar-era films|work=Screen|access-date=30 November 2017|language=en}}</ref> German filmmaker [[Tom Tykwer]] served as Jury President.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cineuropa.org/en/newsdetail/342412/|title=Tom Tykwer to chair the jury of the 68th Berlin Film Festival|website=Cineuropa - the best of european cinema|date=6 November 2017 |access-date=18 September 2019}}</ref>

American film director [[Wes Anderson]]'s animated film ''[[Isle of Dogs (film)|Isle of Dogs]]'' opened the festival, becoming first animated film to open the fest.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://variety.com/2018/film/festivals/berlin-film-festival-wes-anderson-bill-murry-isle-of-dogs-1202700960/|title=Berlin Opening Night: Wes Anderson, Tilda Swinton, Greta Gerwig Celebrate 'Isle of Dogs'|website=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]|date=15 February 2018 |access-date=18 February 2019}}</ref> Romanian film ''[[Touch Me Not]]'' written and directed by [[Adina Pintilie]] won the [[Golden Bear]], which also served as the closing night film.<ref name="TMN">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2018/feb/24/tounch-me-not-fear-of-sexual-intimacy-golden-bear-berlin-film-festival |title=Romanian film about fear of sexual intimacy wins Golden Bear at Berlin film festival |date=25 February 2018 |access-date=25 February 2018 |work=The Guardian}}</ref>

==Juries==
===Main Competition===
* [[Tom Tykwer]], German filmmaker, producer and composer - Jury President<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://variety.com/2017/film/news/tom-tykwer-preside-2018-berlin-film-festival-jury-berlinale-1202605180/|title=Tom Tykwer to Preside Over 2018 Berlin Film Festival Jury|last=Chu|first=Henry|date=2 November 2017|work=Variety|access-date=30 November 2017|language=en-US}}</ref>
* [[Cécile de France]], Belgian actress<ref name="Jury18">{{cite web |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/das_festival/preise_und_juries/preise_internationale_jury/index.html |title=The International Jury 2018 |date=6 February 2018 |access-date=6 February 2018 |archive-date=13 February 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180213193955/https://www.berlinale.de/en/das_festival/preise_und_juries/preise_internationale_jury/index.html |url-status=dead }}</ref>
* Chema Prado, Spanish journalist and film critic
* [[Adele Romanski]], American film producer
* [[Ryuichi Sakamoto]], Japanese musician and composer
* [[Stephanie Zacharek]], American journalist and film critic

===Best First Feature Award===
* [[Jonas Carpignano]], Italian filmmaker<ref name="JURY">{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/archive/jahresarchive/2018/04_jury_2018/04_jury_2018.html|title=JURIES 2018|work=Berlinale|access-date=15 December 2019}}</ref>
* [[Călin Peter Netzer]], Romanian filmmaker and producer
* [[Noa Regev]], Israeli director of the [[Jerusalem Cinematheque]] and the [[Jerusalem Film Festival]]

===Documentary Award===
* Cíntia Gil, Portuguese curator of international festivals and events<ref name="JURY" />
* [[Ulrike Ottinger]], German filmmaker and photographer
* [[Eric Schlosser]], American journalist and writer (United States)

===Short Film Competition===
* Diogo Costa Amarante, Portuguese director and director of photography<ref name="JURY" />
* [[Jyoti Mistry]], South-African director and teacher at the Wits School of Arts in Johannesburg
* Mark Toscano, American curator of international festivals and events

==Official Awards==
[[File:Tom_Tykwer_(Berlin_Film_Festival_2009).jpg|thumb|[[Tom Tykwer]], Jury President|213x213px]]
[[File:Adina Pintilie-9078.jpg|150px|thumb|Romanian director with the [[Golden Bear]] at the 2018 Berlinale]]

The following prizes were awarded:<ref name="Jury18"/><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2018/03_preistrger_2018/03_preistraeger_2018.html |title=Berlinale.Archive. Prize winners 2018. |access-date=27 January 2019 |archive-date=21 March 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190321010336/https://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2018/03_preistrger_2018/03_preistraeger_2018.html |url-status=dead }}</ref>

=== Main Competition ===
* [[Golden Bear]]: ''[[Touch Me Not]]'' by [[Adina Pintilie]]
* [[Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize]]: ''[[Mug (film)|Mug]]'' by [[Małgorzata Szumowska]]
* [[Alfred Bauer Prize]]: ''[[The Heiresses (2018 film)|The Heiresses]]'' by [[Marcelo Martinessi]]
* [[Silver Bear for Best Director]]: [[Wes Anderson]] for ''[[Isle of Dogs (film)|Isle of Dogs]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Actress]]: [[Ana Brun]] for ''[[The Heiresses (2018 film)|The Heiresses]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Actor]]: [[Anthony Bajon]] for ''[[The Prayer (film)|The Prayer]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Screenplay]]: [[Alonso Ruizpalacios]] and Manuel Alcalá for ''[[Museum (2018 film)|Museum]]''
* '''Silver Bear for Outstanding Artistic Contribution''': Elena Okopnaya for ''[[Dovlatov (film)|Dovlatov]]'' (costume and production design)

=== [[Golden Bear for Best Short Film]] ===
* ''The Men Behind the Wall'' by Ines Moldavsky

=== GWFF Best First Feature Award ===
* ''[[Touch Me Not]]'' by [[Adina Pintilie]]

=== Generation KPlus ===
* '''Crystal Bear for Best Short Film''': ''A Field Guide to Being a 12-Year-Old Girl'' by [[Tilda Cobham-Hervey]]<ref>{{cite web |title=Prizes & Honours 2018 |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2018/03_preistrger_2018/03_preistraeger_2018.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190321010336/https://www.berlinale.de/en/archiv/jahresarchive/2018/03_preistrger_2018/03_preistraeger_2018.html |archive-date=21 March 2019 |access-date=2 April 2019 |website=Berlin Film Festival}}</ref>

== Independent Awards ==

=== [[Prize of the Ecumenical Jury]] ===
* ''[[In the Aisles]]'' by Thomas Stuber<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.signis.net/news/culture/26-02-2018/68th-berlinale-awards-of-the-ecumenical-jury|title=68th Berlinale: Awards of the Ecumenical Jury|date=26 February 2018|access-date=27 February 2018}}</ref>
